label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's analyze this question about causation. The question is about whether Sue caused Bill and Sue to end up with the paired set of Bartlett bookends. Here's what we know: 1. Sue is out of town for the weekend. 2. Sue sees a "marvelous bookend" called a Bartlett bookend. It's a right-side bookend, so it would pair with a left-side one. 3. Sue plans to go back and get one because it would be "perfect for the left side of our bookshelf." 4. Bill visits his friend, asks to buy the bookend, is told it's a precious heirloom and can't be sold. 5. Bill steals the bookend by slipping it into his bag while his friend is in the bathroom. 6. Bill leaves with the stolen right-side Bartlett bookend. 7. The next day, Sue buys the left-side Bartlett bookend. 8. When Sue returns home, they now have the paired set of bookends. The question is: Did Sue cause them to possess the paired set of bookends? Let me break this down. Sue's action was: - She saw a (right-side) bookend - She intended to buy a (left-side) bookend to match it - She actually bought the left-side bookend the next day Bill's actions were: - He went to his friend's house
